task: CLM physical ceiling analysis — Mac CPU native path + H100 GPU hexa-native path — MFU breakdown + headroom
verdict: both paths operate <1% MFU today; direct path to ~10-30% MFU on Mac (raw-f32 Tensor + sgemm in nn_core) and ~15-40% on H100 (current D=64 bench is micro, D=512 config achievable after interpreter OOM unblock + grad_accum)
authorization: user 'yes' — pure analysis only, no training launch

mac.notes: M3 base — NOT Max/Studio. Unified memory bandwidth is 100 GB/s (published), not the 400 GB/s of M1/M2 Max. LLC 4 MiB, L1i 128 KiB per core.
mac.ceiling_source: measured from hexa_native_accelerate_spike_20260415.json 512x512 f32 BLAS row = 0.001713s → 2*512^3/t = 156.7 GF/s

bench_train_flag_on_d16.train_flops_per_step_note: 6*N*tok. Kaplan/Hoffmann canonical — 2N fwd + 4N bwd+upd. Excludes phi_cache bookkeeping (small constant).

bench_train_native_microbench.source: bench_train_step_native.hexa (array-only reproduction of decoder layer hot work)
bench_train_native_microbench.measurement_type: forward-only (no backward, no optimizer) — agrees on h[0]/h[15] byte-exact w/ interpreter
bench_train_native_microbench.flops_per_step_note: n_layer=2, forward-only. No 3x training multiplier (bench does not run backward).

h100_gpu_d64_fast.note: 0.82 s/step reported in clm_hexa_gpu.json phase 4 note — INTERPRETED path (hexa pod binary was interpreter, not native codegen). 200 steps total 164s matches.

h100_gpu_d512_full.source: project_clm_hexa_gpu_plan memory config + working CLM smoke at /workspace/clm_smoke
h100_gpu_d512_full.measured_note: 0.18-3 sec/step depending on pod contention per memory project_clm_hexa_gpu_plan

mac_cpu_native.ceiling_note: Using measured 512x512 sgemm Accelerate BLAS throughput as the achievable f32 ceiling. Theoretical NEON+AMX peak is higher but unreachable without tensor-core-equivalent AMX intrinsic bundling; 156.7 GF/s is the highest numeric we can honestly name today.

measurements.name: CLM real step (interpreter, flag ON, d16 nl2 seq8)
measurements.mfu_pct_note: 0.000188% of Accelerate BLAS 156.7 GF/s. Effectively 530000x below physical ceiling.
measurements.dominant_bottleneck: interpreter eval overhead + hexa-list allocation churn in nn_core matmul (O(N^2) list append per tensor_zeros call)
measurements.notes: 27.5x speedup vs historical 27500ms baseline is from phi_cache (not nn_core). Raw compute rate still dominated by stage0 interpreter dispatch cost; a single f32 multiply is ~hundreds of ns in stage0.

measurements.name: CLM phi-block isolated (interpreter, flag ON)
measurements.flops_per_iter_note: phi block does one 64*128 anchor projection + contrastive scoring — ~2*seq*d*d_proj ≈ 2*64*128*12 ≈ 196k flops at seq=64 d=128 proj=12 domains
measurements.note: isolated phi block, not a full step. Speedup metric 630x is vs 27.5s historical WHOLE-STEP. Achieved GFLOP/s is still <0.005, same regime as real step — confirms interpreter dispatch dominates.

measurements.name: CLM phi_cache_loader (NATIVE C via hexa build, seq=64 d=128)
measurements.notes: First NATIVE measurement — 393.8x interpreter, single-core scalar C. Hot loop is still hand-rolled scalar (no BLAS call), so GFLOP/s ≈ measured sgemm-scalar baseline. Headroom = gain sgemm/OpenMP integration → 67x BLAS or 4x OMP further.

measurements.name: CLM full-loop microbench (NATIVE C, 2-layer forward, seq=4 d=16 d_ff=64)
measurements.dominant_bottleneck: tiny config — seq=4 d=16 means matmul kernels are ~16x16 each, too small for sgemm crossover; scalar code is already near single-core compute-bound at this size. Achieved 0.03 GF/s reflects tiny-problem startup overhead (now_ns() exec call per iter, warmup effects, function call overhead), NOT steady-state throughput.
measurements.notes: Non-informative about ceiling at this size. Scale to d=64+ before taking MFU seriously; at d=16 seq=4 a single call to exec('date +%s%N') alone (~ms) dominates. Re-measure at seq=64 d=128 matmul-dominated config to get real MFU.

measurements.name: raw sgemm 512x512 f32 (BLAS baseline, not CLM)
measurements.note: Definitional — this IS the ceiling. Included to show sgemm achieves its measured peak and the gap to CLM numbers is entirely in the path from CLM to this kernel.

remaining_levers.desc: Raw f32 Tensor type primitive landed (4757x vs list-backed matmul). This removes the HEXA_ARRAY_COPY_O_N2 blocker documented in hexa_native_accelerate_spike JSON. MUST be rewired into training/nn_core.hexa matmul hot path next.
remaining_levers.expected_mfu_delta_pct: +30 relative (removes list-alloc moat, BLAS call actually delivers)

remaining_levers.desc: Rewrite training/nn_core.hexa matmul/add/layernorm/softmax to operate on raw f32 buffers (Tensor primitive from L1) and call acc_sgemm_raw from accelerate_blas.hexa via hxblas_wrapper.dylib.
remaining_levers.expected_mfu_delta_pct: +67x single-core (scalar C → BLAS) for any matmul-dominated step at d>=64

remaining_levers.desc: FFI_FLOAT_DOUBLE blocker in hexa_v2 gen2_ffi_c_type. Currently the BLAS path requires hxblas_wrapper.c shim because extern fn X(alpha: Float) emits C double signature while cblas_sgemm takes C float. Fix by adding 'f32' explicit type that maps to C float in gen2_ffi_c_type (self/codegen_c2.hexa:493-504), rebuild hexa_v2.
remaining_levers.expected_mfu_delta_pct: +5-10% (eliminates one function call indirection and dlopen path, enables static FFI)

remaining_levers.status: VERIFIED IN SPIKE — not in hot path yet
remaining_levers.desc: OpenMP 4x speedup measured at 512x512 (9.37 GF/s scalar → BLAS internally uses OMP). Non-BLAS kernels (rmsnorm, softmax, layernorm) should get #pragma omp parallel for on batch/seq outer loops.

bench_train_step_flag_on.hexa killed at 100 steps (target 500) due to stage0 interpreter ~6.7GB RSS from STEP_MS list + model state accumulation. Native compile path dodges this entirely, so priority is NOT to fix interpreter — priority is to finish L1+L2 so native is the default path.
remaining_levers.unlocks: permits 500-step convergence confirmation once native struct-literal codegen is fixed (agent #17)

remaining_levers.desc: hexa_v2 emit_struct_literal in no-arg constructors produces map-backed HexaVal that loses field names. Blocks train_clm_bin whole-binary native step measurement. Fix in self/codegen_c2.hexa emit_struct_literal. Agent #17 scope.
remaining_levers.expected_mfu_delta_pct: unblocks native path for full train_clm.hexa — expected 1-5 ms/step at tiny config per state_json
remaining_levers.unlocks: end-to-end train_step native measurement → replaces interpreter 1000 ms/step with ~2-5 ms/step (200-500x)

remaining_levers.desc: Apple M3 has AMX tile-matrix coprocessor accessible via Accelerate (already used by sgemm). For hand-rolled kernels (rmsnorm, softmax), tile sizing for 128-KiB L1 is the next gain. Marginal (<2x) vs L1-L4 combined.

h100_gpu_hexa.hardware_peak_tflops_note: bf16 SXM. fp32 = 67 TF/s (already 15x below bf16). Training uses bf16 master, so 989 is the right denominator.

measurements.name: CLM d64_fast (D=64 NL=4, interpreted pod binary, 0.82 s/step)
measurements.dominant_bottleneck: interpreter overhead on pod — pod hexa binary is stage0 interpreter, not self-native build. Phase 4 note confirms '~2s/step for D=256 — interpreter overhead is bottleneck'. GPU is idle ~99.99% of step wall time while interpreter marshals launch args.
measurements.notes: D=64 config is also micro enough that cuBLAS launch overhead exceeds compute, so even with zero interpreter overhead MFU would be <1% at D=64 SEQ=512 B=1.

measurements.name: CLM d512 L12 (project_clm_hexa_gpu_plan memory config, 0.18-3 s/step observed)
measurements.dominant_bottleneck: BATCH=1 means tokens/step=512. A single 512x512x512 sgemm on H100 runs at ~200 TF/s, so pure compute bound = 0.78 ms — actual 180 ms is 230x slower → overhead is launch latency × layer count × step, cuBLAS/cuRTC compile, interpreter dispatch, and zero-opt optimizer pass.
measurements.notes: This is our single best CLM GPU data point. 0.09% MFU best-case. The number is not terrible for this stage — it's a 1-batch pure-hexa FFI path through NVRTC-compiled kernels, and the kernel lane is hand-written naive. 100-1000x headroom available.

remaining_levers.desc: Current BATCH=1 means H100 sees tokens/step=512 (tiny). At BATCH=32 SEQ=512 tokens/step=16384 → 32x more flops/step at nearly the same wall time (launch-overhead amortized). Assuming interpreter dispatch stays the same, MFU goes from 0.09% → ~2-3% immediately.
remaining_levers.blocker: activation memory at bf16 d=512 L=12 batch=32 seq=512 ≈ ~1.2 GB per layer × 12 = 14.4 GB — fits on H100 80GB with grad_accum=1. No blocker beyond code.

remaining_levers.desc: Pod hexa binary is old stage0 interpreter. Native compile via hexa build → pod binary would cut per-step wall from ~180 ms → estimated ~10-30 ms (same speedup ratio as Mac 193x native-vs-interp if launch-latency doesn't bottom out first). MFU → ~1-5%.
remaining_levers.blocker: requires L0_stage0_rebuild or copying mac native binary to pod (arch mismatch — mac is arm64, pod is x86_64, so must rebuild in pod)

remaining_levers.desc: Phase 2 cuda_kernels.hexa shipped 12 custom kernels as separate NVRTC modules. Next level: fused QKV (one kernel 3 matmuls), fused SwiGLU+Down, fused backward passes. Each fusion saves 2-3 kernel launches + keeps intermediates in SMEM.

cublasSetMathMode(CUBLAS_TENSOR_OP_MATH) plus bf16 storage on activations — already in evolution_anchors level 6 of clm_hexa_gpu.json. Current path is fp32 sgemm (67 TF/s peak, 7%) not bf16 (989 TF/s peak).
remaining_levers.expected_mfu_delta_pct_relative: peak ceiling changes from 67 → 989 TF/s while kernels still deliver same absolute GF/s. Honest translation: delivered tflops unchanged, but fp32 MFU stats stop being misleading.
remaining_levers.important: This is a METRIC FIX not a speed fix. Need L1+L2 to reach meaningful bf16 utilization.

remaining_levers.desc: flash_attn3.hexa is already shipped in hexa-lang self/ml/. Not wired into gpu_train.hexa forward path yet. At D=512 seq=512 the attention step should drop from O(seq^2 d) to O(seq d + seq^2) in SMEM.
NCCL extern fn bindings → data parallel. 8x H100 pod would give near-linear speedup once L1-L5 land. Out of scope until per-pod MFU is >10%.
remaining_levers.expected_mfu_delta_pct_relative: N/A at this stage — would dilute current absolute flops to 8 GPUs, each at the same fraction. Do NOT pursue until per-GPU MFU is ≥20%.

summary.best_measured_path_by_mfu_pct: Mac native phi_cache_loader spike (1.13% of 156.7 GF/s Accelerate ceiling)
summary.worst_measured_path: CLM real step interpreter (0.0002% of Mac BLAS ceiling = 425000x below 80% target)
summary.ceiling_distance_x_current_best_mac_nati: 3740x from 80% MFU — the 2.09ms microbench is start-dominated, not steady-state
top_3_remaining_levers_cross_path: 1. Mac: Wire Tensor raw f32 primitive (already landed in commit 0c29a5c5) into training/nn_core.hexa matmul and then call acc_sgemm_raw via hxblas_wrapper.dylib. Expected +3000x on matmul-dominated CLM steps (removes O(N^2) list moat + unlocks 156.7 GF/s BLAS).
2. H100: Increase BATCH from 1 to 32 at D=512 L=12 (fits on 80GB, activation ~15GB). Immediate 30x MFU improvement with zero code beyond the batch-axis wiring in gpu_train.hexa forward_full.

3. Both paths: Land agent #17 hexa_v2 emit_struct_literal fix so train_clm.hexa whole-binary native compile runs. Unblocks end-to-end native measurement on Mac (1000 ms → ~2-5 ms/step, 200-500x) and (after rebuild) on pod.
(bonus 4). Agent #18 hexa_v2 FFI float/double fix removes the hxblas_wrapper.c shim and enables direct static extern cblas_sgemm / cublasSgemm_v2 — eliminates dlopen path + shim call indirection.
summary.mfu_honesty_note: All MFU numbers assume f32 peak for Mac (156.7 GF/s measured sgemm) and bf16 peak for H100 (989 TF/s spec). Using fp64 or bf16 ceilings for Mac or fp32 ceilings for H100 would change these absolute percentages but NOT the relative ordering. The qualitative answer is: both paths have 3-4 orders of magnitude of headroom, the Mac path has a clean list-of-6 lever sequence to get to 10-30% MFU, and the H100 path has a shorter 3-lever sequence (batch+native+fusion) to get to 10-20% MFU.
summary.next_biggest_win: Wire Tensor primitive + sgemm into nn_core.hexa. It's the only lever that is (a) already has all its dependencies landed, (b) 100% within reach without cross-repo coordination, and (c) compounds for every subsequent measurement. Conservative expected outcome: CLM train_step at d=64 drops from ~2 s/step interpreter → ~20 ms/step native+BLAS, MFU rises from 0.0002% to 1-3%.

rules_honored.R1_HEXA_FIRST: Pure analysis — no new .py/.rs/.sh files. State JSON only.
rules_honored.L0_untouched: No edits to anima/core/hub, laws, runtime.
rules_honored.no_training_launch: Zero training processes started or touched.
rules_honored.no_pod_commands: Zero ssh/pod commands. All H100 numbers read from memory + roadmap JSON.
rules_honored.honest_gaps: Did not inflate ceiling distances. Where the microbench is start-dominated (seq=4 d=16), state says so explicitly. Where the d64_fast measurement is interpreter-bottlenecked, state says so explicitly.
rules_honored.worktree_isolation: Agent works in agent-a8165f89 worktree branch (anima repo); this state file commits to nexus repo (where shared/ physically lives per reference_shared_layout).
